title: Synapse staining - mouse brain sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"jkef","text":"This protocol details a method to visualize and quantify excitatory synapses using mouse brain sections.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ae1dt","text":"Follow institutional guidelines for the disposal of biological and chemical waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"d8j74","text":"40 μm free-floating mouse brain sections","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hk1u","text":"normal goat serum (NGS; Thermo Fisher, Cat# 01-6201) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ebjf0","text":"rabbit anti-PSD95 (Cat# 51-6900, RRID:AB_2533914; Thermo Fisher Scientific) primary antibody","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"76c5t","text":"guineapig anti-Bassoon (Cat# 141 318, RRID:AB_2927388; Synaptic Systems) primary antibody ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5r122","text":"Alexa Fluor 488 goat anti-rabbit IgG(H+L) (Thermo Fisher, Cat# A-11008, RRID:AB_143165) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ag9th","text":"Alexa Fluor 647 goat anti-guineapig IgG(H+L) secondary antibodies (Thermo Fisher, Cat# A-21450, RRID:AB_2535867) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1l8h7","text":"DAPI (Invitrogen, Cat# D1306)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4sj33","text":"Mowiol (Calbiochem, Cat# 475904)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2t35t","text":"Fluorescence microscope (e.g. Zeiss LSM 880 microscope)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bp9da","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wash the brain sections in 1x TBS containing 0.2% Triton X-100 (TBST). Use 40 μm free-floating mouse brain sections containing the visual cortex.
Block non-specific binding by incubating sections in 10% normal goat serum (NGS) diluted in TBST for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Mount sections onto glass slides using Mowiol.

Incubate sections in Alexa Fluor 488 goat anti-rabbit IgG(H+L) and Alexa Fluor 647 goat anti-guineapig IgG(H+L) secondary antibodies, diluted 1:100 in TBST, for 03:00:00 at Room temperature in the dark.
Incubate sections with rabbit anti-PSD95 (1:300) and guineapig anti-Bassoon (1:500) primary antibodies diluted in blocking buffer (10% NGS in TBST) for 2 nights at 4 °C with gentle shaking.
Wash sections with TBST (3x 10 min). For nuclear staining, add DAPI (1:5000) to the second wash.
Allow slides to cure overnight at Room temperature in the dark.

Acquire high-magnification z-stack images using a fluorescent microscope (e.g. Zeiss LSM 880): Use a 63× objective plus 1.6× optical zoom. Capture 3 optical sections spaced 0.34 μm apart per z-stack. Ensure consistent imaging settings for all conditions. Image within 2 days for optimal results.
Identify excitatory synapses by the colocalization of pre-synaptic (Bassoon) and post-synaptic (PSD95) puncta.
Quantify the number of co-localized synaptic puncta using SynBot, an ImageJ-based software ( https://github.com/Eroglu-Lab/Syn_Bot ).
creator: Sarah van Veen
